sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Let me check what changed after clicking on the green token at [31, 19]. The result says 1 cell changed. Let me compare before and after frames.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Compare before and after to see what changed
before = last_transition.before_frame
after = last_transition.after_frame

# Get segmentation diffs
before_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in before.segmentation['nodes']}
after_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in after.segmentation['nodes']}

# Find changed cells by comparing ascii
before_lines = before.ascii.split('\n')
after_lines = after.ascii.split('\n')
changed = []
for i, (bl, al) in enumerate(zip(before_lines, after_lines)):
    for j, (bc, ac) in enumerate(zip(bl, al)):
        if bc != ac:
            changed.append((i, j, bc, ac))
print(f"Changed cells: {changed}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Only the bottom-right corner changed from 'c' to 'W'. That's part of the black bar at row 63. This seems like a HUD/timer change, not gameplay. The click didn't actually do anything meaningful to the puzzle state.
Wait, but `gameplay_changed` was true. Let me reconsider. The black bar at the bottom (id=13) had 64 pixels of 'c'. Now one pixel changed to 'W'. This could be a countdown/timer bar where each action reduces it by one.
So clicking on the green token didn't move it. Let me try a different approach - maybe I need to use arrow keys to move something. Let me try pressing RIGHT to see if one of the green tokens moves.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# The click only changed the bottom bar (HUD). Let me try arrow keys to move the green tokens.
# First, let me try RIGHT to see if something moves
action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
